topStory.text: PJM's 2028/29 capacity auction cleared at the FERC-approved cap of $325/MW-day (UCAP) — procuring 138,318 MW for a $16.4 billion total — and for the first time in PJM history the entire RTO fell short of its reliability requirement, by 6,831 MW. Surging data-center demand is the primary driver of the price run-up now flowing through to consumer bills.

feed.text: PowerPlay AI announced a 400 MW behind-the-meter AI data center development in greater Abilene, Texas through a joint venture with a Nasdaq-listed neocloud partner — targeting initial power in 2028, the latest ERCOT project to bypass the interconnection queue with on-site generation.

QTS and Lancium announced a $10+ billion data center campus in Hall County, Texas — up to 11 QTS data centers across ~465 acres on Lancium's Clean Campus near Turkey, anchored by a 1 GW grid connection, with the companies committing to pay for all needed energy-infrastructure upgrades themselves.

Crusoe and Lancium announced a 1.0 GW grid-connected AI data center campus in Childress, Texas on a 270-acre Lancium-owned site, with construction beginning Q3 2026 — adding another gigawatt-scale campus to the ERCOT pipeline.

TSMC announced an additional $100 billion investment in its Phoenix, Arizona campus — four more 2nm-class fabs, lifting total planned US investment to $265 billion, the largest foreign direct investment in US history — after AI demand drove record Q2 profit and a raised capex outlook.

New York Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order creating the nation’s first statewide moratorium on new hyperscale AI data centers — a one-year halt on permits for facilities over 20 MW — citing grid capacity limits and the risk of AI-driven power costs being passed to ratepayers.

The Texas PUC unanimously approved 'ride-through' rules requiring data centers and other large computational loads in ERCOT to stay connected through voltage and frequency disturbances, citing 28 trip events of 100+ MW since 2023 and a 438 GW large-load study queue.

A new Fortune analysis finds data centers have already added an estimated $23 billion to Americans' electricity bills, intensifying the who-pays fight as Virginia's first-of-its-kind $0.011/kWh data-center consumption tax took effect July 1.

Meta announced its Hyperion campus in Richland Parish, Louisiana will be expanded to 5 GW and more than $50 billion — up from a $10B estimate at its December 2024 groundbreaking — making it the largest single data-center campus announced in the US, with 2 GW targeted by 2030.
